intro: Pilea australis, also known as Australian pepper plant, is a plant species native to the eastern coast of Australia, characterized by its small white or yellowish flowers and distinctive red berries. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various conditions such as arthritis and skin infections. Pilea australis contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
